title: Secure HR cases with targeted ACL design
question: How do you secure HR cases so only HR agents and appropriate fulfillers can access them?
summary: HRSD security scenario with table and field access considerations.
scenario: The client wants HR cases to be visible only to authorized HR agents and a limited set of fulfillers. Sensitive fields must also be protected.
exactAnswer: Implement the security model with dedicated HR roles, table and field ACLs, any required criteria based on assignment or ownership, and impersonation-based testing. Protect fields separately if some HR users can open a case but should not see all values.
steps: Define the target HR roles and which personas need read versus write access.
Create or update table ACLs on the HR case table to permit only the allowed roles or ownership-based logic.
Create field ACLs for especially sensitive fields such as investigation details or personal data if required.
Test with impersonation for each persona: HR agent, fulfiller, non-HR user, and admin where appropriate.
Document any exceptions or delegated access models.
validationChecklist: Authorized HR users can see the case.
Unauthorized users cannot open the record.
Sensitive fields remain protected even when the case itself is visible to a broader persona.
commonMistakes: Protecting only the table and forgetting field-level security.
Testing only with admin instead of impersonated end-user personas.
